records.ety: [L. cornu copiae horn of plenty. See Horn, and Copious.]

defs: The horn of plenty, from which fruits and flowers are represented as issuing. It is an emblem of abundance.
A genus of grasses bearing spikes of flowers resembling the cornucopia in form.
notes: ☞ Some writers maintain that this word should be written, in the singular, cornu copiæ, and in the plural, cornua copiæ.
